title: cornus fragifera
intro: Cornus fragifera, also known as the dog's berry or European dogwood, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its white or pale pink flowers and red or yellow berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been utilized to treat various health issues, including wounds, fever, and digestive problems. The medicinal properties of Cornus fragifera are believed to provide several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support overall health and well-being.
